title: The United States of America is the greatest country in the whole world

transcript.text: Hello, this video is America's the best country in the world. Ha ha.
Not England, not Canada, not Australia, not France, not India, not China, not Mexico, not the Bahamas.
America's the best country in the world.
Um, I know some people are like, oh, everybody's, every country's good, you shouldn't say one country's better or worse, but, you know.
Um, if so many people hate America and say America sucks, then maybe there needs to be a little bit of a backlash where you go, actually, uh-uh, America doesn't suck.
After all, how could America suck?
If you say America sucks, that's kind of like saying, if you throw all the cultures in the world together into one country, then that country will suck.
And that doesn't make any sense.
The idea is that if you throw everybody together, you're going to get something beautiful, you're going to get something that's greater than the sum of its parts.
And that's exactly what America is.
It's something that, it's a culture that's greater than the sum of all the little cultures that throw it together.
Because you get a special amalgamation of, um, the best things of all the cultures.
And then we throw the nastiness away and the bad stuff away because, you know, old cultures that have been around a long time from, like, the old world, they have a lot of bad karma to them.
Um, Europe has a lot of bad karma.
There was a lot of evil stuff going on.
I mean, and there's, obviously, with slavery, America had a lot of bad stuff, too.
But, um, but Europe has lots of bad stuff going on for a long time and it even permeates into today, you know, like Europeans or, like English, they're very class-oriented still.
And, uh, you know, you can even tell by someone's accent how much money they have.
And they always like to complain about, oh, this guy has money, oh, he's rich, but he's not like us.
And Americans aren't like that, you know.
We go to public schools and both the rich and the poor go to public schools together and hang out together.
And, uh, people of different, um, styles, you know, like the punks and the, and the greasers and all this stuff, they all kind of hang out together more here than, I think, other places.
Even though there's lots of different cliques in America, whereas in maybe other countries, there are not lots of different cliques.
But that's just because in other countries, in a school, it was all one group of people in that school.
But in America, in a big public school, you've got all the groups of people together.
Because you don't have, like, you know, school for the rich and neighborhoods for the rich and neighborhoods for the poor.
You know, all the neighborhoods are mixed in America.
At least where I came from.
So, um, America also doesn't have, so, you know, for that reason, America doesn't have, like, lots of negative karma.
Which I think can hold on and make a country sticky, negatively sticky and yucky and make people angry.
And I've traveled a lot.
I've traveled, I've been out of the country ten years.
I was in Chile five years.
I was in other countries five years.
Passed through, like, four, three countries.
And I have noticed that people, a lot of people, just don't have been angry with me and not like me just because I'm American.
Like, in Australia, they go, you know, Australia's the best country in the world.
I'm like, all right.
And then they tell you again, Australia's the best country in the world, you know.
Like, an American would never tell an Australian, hey, man, America's the best country.
At least I've never seen it.
I mean, I haven't traveled around America that much.
Not at all.
But, um, but, um, Americans, you know, when a foreign person comes here, we're like, cool, foreign person.
Instead of like, ugh.
You know, like the French, they look at you and they go, me, American.
Um, and I've seen that a bunch.
I'm not going to go into stories, but yeah.
Like, once I was at his truck stop and this guy goes, heard me talking, he goes, where are you from?
I go, American.
He goes to his friend, eh, American.
And they all roll their eyes.
And I was like, so I just wrote on.
But, um, and I've seen that happen everywhere I go.
People not like me for that.
But, um, but we're not like that.
We just love everybody.
And, uh, America, you know, if you look at our culture, I think another reason Americans, uh, people don't like Americans is because they're jealous of us.
It's because our culture is so strong and takes over so many other countries.
Like, you go to any, any country.
And of all the countries I've been to, all the most vast majority, like almost all of the movies in the movie theaters are American movies.
Because the only country that I saw that wasn't like that was Malaysia.
And that was kind of weird because Malaysians speak like perfect English.
But, um, and also you turn on the radio and it's American music too.
It's like flooding it.
Like more American music than their music.
And like every radio I've heard in every country I've been to, because I'm a big radio connoisseur when I go to those places.
And, uh, and then TV shows and, uh, and then, so I think maybe that's that.
That would be the reason people, like, don't like Americans.
And then there's the whole military thing.
But as far as I'm concerned, there has to be somebody policing the world.
Because there's lots of countries that I just don't trust.
After traveling to countries, I don't trust, I don't ever want to work in another country again because I just don't trust people there.
And I'm not going to go into stories, examples, because I don't have the time and I don't want to come across like I'm complaining and stuff.
But there has to be somebody policing the world until we can all agree that we have a higher police force out there, the aliens.
But we haven't agreed on that yet.
So, you know, until then we just have to pretend that, you know, we have to watch our own backs.
And if we as a planet have to watch our own backs, then we have to have a police force.
So that's America.
And we pay for it with our tax money, you know.
We sacrifice a...
We could have a good health care system if we didn't spend money on the military.
So we sacrifice ourselves to have a strong military as far as I'm concerned.
So we're paying for that military.
When we go into Iraq, it's the Americans who are sacrificing.
And we're also sacrificing on blood, too, with our troops.
So, you know, to say that it's all about control and power and evil American people and evil American government, that doesn't make sense to me because we sacrifice ourselves to help these countries.
And, you know, some people might say, oh, we went into Iraq for oil.
Well, maybe that's why Bush went into Iraq, but that's not why the troops went into Iraq and why 69% of American populace wanted to go into Iraq when we went into Iraq.
We wanted to go into Iraq because we didn't like Iraq, because we didn't think it should be a country.
Because they...
And we were afraid that they were going to try and shoot nuclear missiles and stuff, so...
And we want a world that's together, so that's why.
So, you know, some people say, oh, Americans are a closed society.
We don't know anything about the rest of the world.
And that's true, but why should we?
We haven't.
We know enough about ourselves.
We know enough about the cultures of the rest of the world that are within our borders.
We don't have to know about all the crap that's happening in other countries.
You know, why would you pay attention to crap?
We would only want to pay attention to positive stuff.
And if all a country has to share is crap, then we're not going to pay attention to it.
We're just going to pay attention to ourselves.
You know, we only have enough time, a certain amount of hours in a day to pay attention to news and stuff and culture.
And so we're going to pay attention to ourselves.
And that's why we're, you know, enclosed in that sense.
But as far as envisioning one world, it's Americans who are the most open.
And that's why, that's the very reason why we go and invade other countries like Afghanistan, Iraq, and spread our culture.
And we, I think we have the most eclectic, cosmopolitan-type culture on the whole planet.
But there's other countries that, you know, if you go down a road, you're going to see a Mexican restaurant here.
You're in, well, not as many, you know, like in Peru, you'll see Chinese restaurants and stuff.

But I don't know any other places, maybe London, but like in America, every town in America, you go down here, Chinese restaurant here, Arabic restaurant down here, Greek restaurant right next to it, then Italian restaurant, then this, then that, then the other thing.
So, and it's all there on the same road, and you're not going to get that anywhere else.
So, that's, it's the great melting pot, isn't it?
So, I'm just defending my country, because I'm American, and I say America's the best country in the world.
Best country in the world, as in the country that is most representative of a utopian world society.
Best country in the world, as in the country that other countries should follow and should try to emulate.
That's what I mean, as in best country in the world.
The rightful leader of the whole world, culturally.

So, it's no accident, and it's no, uh, and it's a good thing that, um, that we're the leaders, you know, and if, and, and if some people go, America's time will come and go, you know, I'd be like, I'd love it if America's time came and went, because that would mean that another country is better.
And we'll take over, and if it, if it's like that, then that means it's a good country, and I want to visit it, you know.
I'd rather visit a, a good Russia or China than a fucked up Russia or China, and that's what they are right now, so.
Anyways, I'm out of time.
America's the best country in the world, better than all the rest.
